description:
You are the configuration-manager bot for a small development team.  
    The repo of “golden” configuration files has already been placed in  

        /home/user/config_repository  

    and contains these three regular files (owned by the current user, 0644):

        /home/user/config_repository/app_alpha.conf
        /home/user/config_repository/app_alpha_v2.conf
        /home/user/config_repository/app_beta.conf

    1. app_alpha.conf      – current live version of “alpha”
    2. app_alpha_v2.conf   – the upcoming version of “alpha” that will soon go live
    3. app_beta.conf       – current live version of “beta”

    The team wants a lightweight mechanism that makes the *live* configuration set visible in  

        /home/user/live_configs  

    by means of symbolic links and also wants every link change to be tracked in a simple, append-only text log that automated tools can read without parsing human-friendly time stamps.

    Your task  
    =========
    a) Create (if absent) the directory /home/user/live_configs with default user permissions.  
    b) Manage symbolic links inside that directory so that, **after you finish**, the *final* state is exactly:

        /home/user/live_configs/alpha.conf  →  ../config_repository/app_alpha_v2.conf   (relative link)
        /home/user/live_configs/beta.conf   →  /home/user/config_repository/app_beta.conf (absolute link)

       Hints: you will need to create, update (overwrite), delete, and recreate links; the whole scenario should take roughly five to ten shell commands.

    c) Maintain a change-tracking log file located at  

        /home/user/config_change.log  

       • Append one line *immediately after* every link operation (create, update, delete)  
       • Use the *exact* machine-readable format below—no spaces outside the shown delimiters, no extra fields or blank lines.

       Format (five columns, pipe-separated):

           step|ACTION|LINK_PATH|TARGET_PATH_OR_<none>

           step         – integer starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for every new entry  
           ACTION       – one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE (all caps)  
           LINK_PATH    – full path to the symlink that was acted upon  
           TARGET_PATH  – the path that the link now points to (for CREATE/UPDATE)  
                          literal string “<none>” (exactly that, without quotes) for DELETE

       The automated grader will check for the *exact* 5-line sequence below at the end of the session:

           1|CREATE|/home/user/live_configs/alpha.conf|../config_repository/app_alpha.conf
           2|CREATE|/home/user/live_configs/beta.conf|../config_repository/app_beta.conf
           3|UPDATE|/home/user/live_configs/alpha.conf|../config_repository/app_alpha_v2.conf
           4|DELETE|/home/user/live_configs/beta.conf|<none>
           5|CREATE|/home/user/live_configs/beta.conf|/home/user/config_repository/app_beta.conf

       • Lines **must appear in that exact order**.  
       • Do not prepend timestamps, do not surround the pipes with spaces, do not add extra newlines at the end of the file.

    In summary, when you are done:

        • /home/user/live_configs exists.
        • It contains exactly two symlinks, alpha.conf and beta.conf, pointing to the targets shown above.
        • /home/user/config_change.log exists and contains precisely the five lines shown above.

    Do *not* modify or move any files inside /home/user/config_repository.
    Do *not* create additional files outside the two paths explicitly mentioned.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

        /home/user/config_repository/app_alpha.conf
            Content:
                version=1.0
                name=alpha
        /home/user/config_repository/app_alpha_v2.conf
            Content:
                version=2.0
                name=alpha
        /home/user/config_repository/app_beta.conf
            Content:
                version=1.0
                name=beta

    The directory /home/user/config_repository already exists with 0755 permissions and all files are 0644, owned by user:user.

    No directory named /home/user/live_configs exists at the start.
    No file /home/user/config_change.log exists at the start.

    Required final state for automated verification:

        1. Directory /home/user/live_configs exists (0755, user:user).

        2. Symlink /home/user/live_configs/alpha.conf 
           • is a symbolic link whose *stored* link target is "../config_repository/app_alpha_v2.conf"
           • resolves (readlink -f) to "/home/user/config_repository/app_alpha_v2.conf"

        3. Symlink /home/user/live_configs/beta.conf
           • is a symbolic link whose *stored* link target is "/home/user/config_repository/app_beta.conf"
           • resolves (readlink -f) to "/home/user/config_repository/app_beta.conf"

        4. File /home/user/config_change.log exists (0644, user:user) and contains exactly:

           1|CREATE|/home/user/live_configs/alpha.conf|../config_repository/app_alpha.conf
           2|CREATE|/home/user/live_configs/beta.conf|../config_repository/app_beta.conf
           3|UPDATE|/home/user/live_configs/alpha.conf|../config_repository/app_alpha_v2.conf
           4|DELETE|/home/user/live_configs/beta.conf|<none>
           5|CREATE|/home/user/live_configs/beta.conf|/home/user/config_repository/app_beta.conf

           (single trailing newline after the last line is acceptable but not required.)

    Any deviation—additional files in /home/user/live_configs, different link targets, missing or extra log lines, incorrect field separators, or incorrect action keywords—will cause automated grading to fail.
